name: OpenCut
readme_summary: OpenCut is a free and open-source video editor being rewritten from the ground up to support web, desktop, and mobile platforms with a Rust core. It aims to offer a plugin-first architecture, an editor API, and headless capabilities for automation and batch rendering.
key_features: Cross-platform (web, desktop, mobile) with Rust core
primary_use_case: A cross-platform, open-source video editing application.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a bustling workshop, using a magical, multi-faceted lens to sculpt raw video footage into a polished cinematic masterpiece. Raw, unedited film reels are fed into one side of the lens, transforming into vibrant, dynamic scenes projected onto a large screen on the other side. Subtle UI elements of timelines and editing tools are integrated into the workshop's machinery. The OpenCut logo is subtly etched onto the artisan's apron. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a warm, creative atmosphere and clear, defined elements.

name: CLI-Anything
description: "CLI-Anything: Making ALL Software Agent-Native" -- CLI-Hub: https://clianything.cc/
readme_summary: CLI-Anything is a Python-based framework designed to make all software accessible to AI agents. It provides a unified hub for installing and managing command-line interfaces (CLIs), enabling AI agents to interact with and control various applications and services seamlessly.
key_features: CLI-Hub for browsing, installing, and managing community-built CLIs
Support for various AI agents (Pi, OpenClaw, nanobot, Cursor, Claude Code, etc.)
Unified skill management with `skills/` directory and `npx skills add` command
primary_use_case: To enable AI agents to interact with and control any software through a unified command-line interface system.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic robot holding a universal remote control, standing in front of a vast digital landscape filled with diverse software icons (like gears, code brackets, design tools, and game controllers). The robot is pointing the remote, and from its tip, streams of light are connecting to and activating these software icons, causing them to glow. Subtle UI elements showing command inputs and outputs float around the robot. The overall mood is one of seamless integration and empowerment, with a clean, modern digital art style and vibrant, inviting colors.

name: annyang
readme_summary: annyang is a lightweight JavaScript library that enables speech recognition on websites, allowing users to control web applications and interact with content using voice commands. It requires no external dependencies and is easy to integrate.
primary_use_case: Enabling users to control websites and web applications using voice commands.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot assistant with a microphone icon on its chest, actively listening. Around the robot, speech bubbles transform into actionable icons like a play button, a search magnifying glass, and a navigation arrow, representing voice commands being translated into website actions. Subtle digital sound waves emanate from the robot. The background is a clean, modern website interface with a soft glow. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with bright, friendly colors, clearly showing 'annyang' subtly integrated into a small UI element on the robot's screen.

name: promises-unwrapping
description: The ES6 promises spec, as per September 2013 TC39 meeting
readme_summary: This repository contains the specification for ES6 Promises, defining their states (fulfilled, rejected, pending) and behavior. It serves as a staging ground for refining the promises specification and fixing related issues, aiming to provide a robust foundation for asynchronous operations in JavaScript.
key_features: Definition of Promise states: fulfilled, rejected, pending
Explanation of Promise resolution and settlement
Abstract operations for Promise management (PromiseCapability, PromiseReaction)
Detailed algorithm steps for Promise handling (IfAbruptRejectPromise)
primary_use_case: Defining and refining the specification for JavaScript Promises to standardize asynchronous programming.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old clockmaker meticulously assembling a complex, multi-layered clockwork mechanism. Each gear and spring represents a different stage of a deferred computation. Some gears are smoothly turning (fulfilled), others are stuck (rejected), and many are waiting to engage (pending). Small, glowing threads of light connect the gears, symbolizing the flow of data and control. The clockmaker uses a magnifying glass that shows a simplified representation of a promise chain. The repository name 'promises-unwrapping' is subtly etched onto the clockmaker's workbench. The scene is set in a cozy, well-lit workshop with a vintage aesthetic. 3D isometric illustration style with a warm, inviting color palette and intricate details.

name: eidos
description: An extensible framework for Personal Data Management.
readme_summary: Eidos is an extensible framework for personal data management, transforming SQLite into a user-friendly pocket database. It offers Notion-like document and database capabilities with strong offline support and integrated AI features for data interaction and extension generation.
key_features: Personal Data Management with Notion-like documents and databases
Offline Support with local data storage
AI Features for translation, summarization, and data interaction
Extensible framework with a simple extension system
primary_use_case: To provide a local-first, extensible, and AI-enhanced personal data management system that functions like a pocket database.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic SQLite database character, depicted as a wise old librarian, is meticulously organizing a vast, colorful library of personal documents and notes. The librarian uses a magical magnifying glass that reveals AI-generated summaries and translations of the documents. Small, glowing digital particles representing data flow from scattered notes into organized shelves. Subtle UI elements resembling Eidos's interface are visible on a nearby desk, showing data organization and extension creation tools. The scene is set in a cozy, well-lit study with a hint of futuristic technology. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a warm color palette and clear, friendly details, emphasizing ease of use and powerful organization.
